title: Technical deep-dive scheduling (invite not received; timezone sync)

Hi Marta —

Great to meet yesterday — thank you for the honest feedback on the use case. I tried to push the calendar invite for the technical deep-dive but it looks like it didn’t arrive on your side. Two quick asks so we can lock this in:

1) Can you confirm your timezone (I have CET in our notes but wanted to be sure)?
2) Would you prefer Webex or Google Meet for the bridge? We can also provide a dedicated Redwood bridge if you want an isolated meeting URL.

Avery Johnson

title: Reseller entitlement exceptions — mapping edge cases and remediation plan
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@resellco.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Patel <maya.patel@resellco.ai>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Entitlement mapping question for AWS PPPO - ResellCo

Hi Evelyn,

Quick one — we're validating SKU-to-entitlement mappings for the private offer we just created for Acme Retail. A couple of SKUs are showing as "non-entitled" in AWS (they're nested add-ons that were supposed to inherit entitlement from the parent SKU). This blocks provisioning for a handful of customers.

Can Redwood confirm whether the PPPO entitlement inheritance should flow automatically, or do we need a manual entity mapping for these sub-SKUs? Attached is our current entitlement matrix (entitlement_matrix_v3.xlsx) with the rows highlighted.

We're trying to avoid a full re-issuance of the private offer if possible — any guidance or a temporary remediation we can ship this week would be hugely helpful.

Thanks — appreciate a short note on next steps.

— Liam
ResellCo Commercial Ops


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@resellco.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Entitlement mapping question for AWS PPPO - ResellCo

Hi Liam — thanks for the matrix and the clear highlights. Short answer: some nested SKUs inherit entitlement automatically, but there are known edge cases when a parent SKU was updated after the private offer was generated or when the SKU ID in the PPPO mapping doesn't match the marketplace listing ID.

Next steps I propose:
- I'll run a quick entitlement audit for the flagged SKUs to confirm whether this is an inheritance bug or an ID mismatch.
- If it's an ID mismatch, we can patch the PPPO mapping (no offer re-issue).
- If it's inheritance/batching behavior, we have a short remediation we can apply (manual entitlement push) to unblock customers while we roll a fix.

Ben/Vanessa — looping you in for ops/legal implications (Ben for private-offer ops; Vanessa for partner billing). Can one of you confirm whether we need any special approvals to push entitlements on ResellCo's behalf?

Liam — I'll have preliminary audit notes by EOD tomorrow PST.

Thanks,
Evelyn
Head of Marketplace Partnerships
Redwood Inference


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 02 Dec 2026 08:22:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Entitlement mapping question for AWS PPPO - ResellCo

Folks — forwarding ResellCo thread. TL;DR: three sub-SKUs are not inheriting entitlement; attachments show mapping. Need ops path + legal check if we do a manual entitlement push.

Can ops provide: an ETA for a manual entitlement push for up to 50 accounts, and a short checklist of audit logs we will record?
Can legal confirm if we should have a DocuSign acknowledgment from ResellCo for entitlement changes to avoid chargebacks?

Attaching the ResellCo matrix here for your convenience.

Attachment: entitlement_matrix_v3.xlsx

— Evelyn


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 03 Dec 2026 10:15:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Entitlement mapping question for AWS PPPO - ResellCo

Evelyn — dug into infra/ops. Quick notes:
- We can do a manual entitlement push for the highlighted SKUs; should take ~2–3 hours to run and validate for up to 50 accounts. We'll log the change-IDs and snapshot pre/post entitlement state for audit.
- We need a signed authorization from ResellCo to proceed (standard ops policy). A short acknowledgement PDF or DocuSign is fine — ops will not proceed without it.

I've drafted a minimal authorization template and attached redlines for legal to review.

Attachment: legal_redline_20261204.pdf

Vanessa — does partner billing need any special note for downstream chargebacks or allocation corrections?

Ben
Director, Platform Ops
Redwood Inference


From: Maya Patel <maya.patel@resellco.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@resellco.ai>, ResellCo Commercial Ops <ops@resellco.ai>
Date: Fri, 04 Dec 2026 14:42:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Entitlement mapping question for AWS PPPO - ResellCo

Thanks team — we reviewed the attachments and legal_redline_20261204.pdf looks good. Liam signed the DocuSign template and I uploaded a signed acknowledgement to the HubSpot deal record (link below). Please proceed with the manual entitlement push.

HubSpot record: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/DL-2026-119
DocuSign: https://www.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?env=na1&acct=0000000000000000&po=12345

Can you confirm a scheduled window? Prefer weekday early morning PST to minimize customer impact.

— Maya
Head of Commercial Ops, ResellCo


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Patel <maya.patel@resellco.ai>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@resellco.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 08 Dec 2026 16:42:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Entitlement mapping question for AWS PPPO - ResellCo — scheduled remediation

Maya/Liam — thanks for the quick turnaround and for signing. Ops will run the manual entitlement push on Thu 2026-12-10 at 06:00 PST. Runbook and validation checklist attached. We will: 
- Snapshot pre-state at 05:45 PST
- Apply entitlement mapping patch at 06:00 PST
- Run validation tests across a sample set of 50 accounts (06:30–07:15 PST)
- Post-change snapshot and evidence uploaded to the HubSpot deal and ops S3 folder.

Vanessa — please confirm any billing allocation notes we should include in the post-change ticket.

Attachments: entitlement_matrix_v3.xlsx, legal_redline_20261204.pdf

Best,
Evelyn
Head of Marketplace Partnerships
Redwood Inference

title: Request: strategic commercial guardrails and discount / MFN request
preview:
From: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@finmetric.com
Date: 2026-12-06T08:22:00-08:00
Subject: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Ben —

Really enjoyed yesterday's demo. FinMetric is moving quickly and we have an internal budget window that closes mid-December. To move forward we need commercial clarity on a couple of non-standard items before procurement can sign:

- 40% introductory discount off list for year 1 (we anticipate 150k ARR initially).
- MFN clause tied to benchmark pricing/perf: if another vendor offers better unit pricing or published perf benchmark for comparable latency/throughput we expect parity or credit.
- 12-month exclusivity in the North American "Enterprise Accounting" vertical (we'd like Redwood to agree not to pursue other named enterprise accounting customers in the segment).

I'm attaching a short SOW and our procurement checklist. Can we get an answer by EOD Tuesday? Procurement is on a tight cadence and we have a pilot planned for January.

Thanks —
Elena Ortiz
Head of Strategic Partnerships
FinMetric Analytics
Attachment: FinMetric_SOW.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T10:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Elena —

Appreciate the quick follow-up and the clear ask. I'll loop in Deal Desk and Marcus on this thread so we can surface risks and get an exec decision. A few quick clarifying Qs that will help speed review:

1) The 40% — is that applied to all Redwood services in scope or just the inference hosting line-item in the SOW?
2) For MFN: are you asking for price parity only (pricing published to FinMetric) or parity against any customer/partner discount we offer?
3) For exclusivity: are you requesting a market carve (enterprise accounting segment only) or a named-customer restriction?

We're attaching our proposed-pricing spreadsheet for reference. Lauren — can you advise on the MFN/exclusivity risk matrix and what approvals we need? Marcus — flagging for potential margin impact and quota treatment.

Next steps: Lauren to provide redline recommendations. If exec signoff needed we'll escalate to Marcus.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachment: proposed-pricing.xlsx


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Date: 2026-12-07T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Team —

Summary of commercial/legal risk after initial review of FinMetric asks (see attached internal memo):

- 40% discount: within bounds for competitive pilot deals but requires approval above Deal Desk floor if applied to recurring list across all products. Recommend capping at 25% for bundled SKUs; 40% only for one-time professional services or a usage-based credit structure tied to milestones.

- MFN (benchmark pricing/perf): This is high risk. MFN that references "published benchmark" or "better unit pricing" can be open-ended and create perpetual obligation. Recommended approach: narrow the MFN to (a) direct list price published to FinMetric, (b) limited duration (12 months from signing), and (c) exclude marketplace/reseller channels and channel promotion discounts. We should also define what counts as a "benchmark" (specific test harness and workload).

- Exclusivity: 12 months full exclusivity across NA enterprise accounting is significant. Recommend instead: exclusivity limited to two named SKUs that are directly tied to the pilot, or a 6–9 month limited co-sell exclusivity for direct-sold enterprise accounts introduced by FinMetric. Also recommend a termination-for-convenience carve if performance targets are not met.

Ask for exec approval on the following recommended compromise:
- Approve up to 30% discount for Year 1 on the inference hosting line-item only (not across all product lines), with discounts ramping down in Year 2.
- Narrow MFN to direct list-price parity for 12 months and explicitly exclude marketplace/reseller pricing; include a defined benchmark test doc (we can attach a performance spec).
- Limit exclusivity to two SKUs and 9 months.

Attachments: term-sheet-redline.docx, RiskMemo_v1.pdf

Ben/Marcus — please advise whether you want me to proceed with a redline to FinMetric along the above or hold for different exec direction. If Marcus prefers, we can present the alternative floor (25% cap, 6 months exclusivity).

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Head of Deal Desk | Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Date: 2026-12-08T16:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails (EXEC DECISION)

Ben / Lauren / Elena —

Thanks for the analysis, Lauren, and for surfacing the ask, Ben. Exec decision below — I'm approving the deal to keep momentum but with specific, enforceable guardrails:

1) Discount: approve a 30% discount on the inference hosting line-item for Year 1 only. Discounts do not apply to professional services or add-on modules unless separately approved.

2) MFN: approve a narrowly scoped MFN limited to direct list-price parity for comparable published unit pricing, effective for 12 months from signature. Explicitly exclude marketplace/reseller programs, promotional campaign discounts, and partner-level rebates. Any benchmark/performance-based claims must be validated against Redwood's benchmark harness (we will provide the test spec).

3) Exclusivity: approve limited exclusivity covering two named SKUs (to be enumerated in the SOW) for 9 months. No blanket category or named-customer restrictions. All exclusivity language must include performance exit clauses and a defined SLA (availability + latency targets) that FinMetric can test against.

4) Risk controls: cap SLA credits at 10% of the annual contract value; require Legal to include an MFN sunset and audit rights limited to published receipts. Finance to confirm revenue recognition and quota treatment.

Lauren — proceed to incorporate these changes into the term sheet and send a redline to FinMetric. Ben — share the updated term sheet and the benchmark test spec when ready; ask FinMetric to confirm acceptance by EOD Wednesday to preserve the procurement cadence.

Marcus Lin
VP Sales | Redwood Inference
text:
From: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@finmetric.com
Date: 2026-12-06T08:22:00-08:00
Subject: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Ben —

Really enjoyed yesterday's demo. FinMetric is moving quickly and we have an internal budget window that closes mid-December. To move forward we need commercial clarity on a couple of non-standard items before procurement can sign:

- 40% introductory discount off list for year 1 (we anticipate 150k ARR initially).
- MFN clause tied to benchmark pricing/perf: if another vendor offers better unit pricing or published perf benchmark for comparable latency/throughput we expect parity or credit.
- 12-month exclusivity in the North American "Enterprise Accounting" vertical (we'd like Redwood to agree not to pursue other named enterprise accounting customers in the segment).

I'm attaching a short SOW and our procurement checklist. Can we get an answer by EOD Tuesday? Procurement is on a tight cadence and we have a pilot planned for January.

Thanks —
Elena Ortiz
Head of Strategic Partnerships
FinMetric Analytics
Attachment: FinMetric_SOW.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T10:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Elena —

Appreciate the quick follow-up and the clear ask. I'll loop in Deal Desk and Marcus on this thread so we can surface risks and get an exec decision. A few quick clarifying Qs that will help speed review:

1) The 40% — is that applied to all Redwood services in scope or just the inference hosting line-item in the SOW?
2) For MFN: are you asking for price parity only (pricing published to FinMetric) or parity against any customer/partner discount we offer?
3) For exclusivity: are you requesting a market carve (enterprise accounting segment only) or a named-customer restriction?

We're attaching our proposed-pricing spreadsheet for reference. Lauren — can you advise on the MFN/exclusivity risk matrix and what approvals we need? Marcus — flagging for potential margin impact and quota treatment.

Next steps: Lauren to provide redline recommendations. If exec signoff needed we'll escalate to Marcus.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachment: proposed-pricing.xlsx


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Date: 2026-12-07T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Team —

Summary of commercial/legal risk after initial review of FinMetric asks (see attached internal memo):

- 40% discount: within bounds for competitive pilot deals but requires approval above Deal Desk floor if applied to recurring list across all products. Recommend capping at 25% for bundled SKUs; 40% only for one-time professional services or a usage-based credit structure tied to milestones.

- MFN (benchmark pricing/perf): This is high risk. MFN that references "published benchmark" or "better unit pricing" can be open-ended and create perpetual obligation. Recommended approach: narrow the MFN to (a) direct list price published to FinMetric, (b) limited duration (12 months from signing), and (c) exclude marketplace/reseller channels and channel promotion discounts. We should also define what counts as a "benchmark" (specific test harness and workload).

- Exclusivity: 12 months full exclusivity across NA enterprise accounting is significant. Recommend instead: exclusivity limited to two named SKUs that are directly tied to the pilot, or a 6–9 month limited co-sell exclusivity for direct-sold enterprise accounts introduced by FinMetric. Also recommend a termination-for-convenience carve if performance targets are not met.

Ask for exec approval on the following recommended compromise:
- Approve up to 30% discount for Year 1 on the inference hosting line-item only (not across all product lines), with discounts ramping down in Year 2.
- Narrow MFN to direct list-price parity for 12 months and explicitly exclude marketplace/reseller pricing; include a defined benchmark test doc (we can attach a performance spec).
- Limit exclusivity to two SKUs and 9 months.

Attachments: term-sheet-redline.docx, RiskMemo_v1.pdf

Ben/Marcus — please advise whether you want me to proceed with a redline to FinMetric along the above or hold for different exec direction. If Marcus prefers, we can present the alternative floor (25% cap, 6 months exclusivity).

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Head of Deal Desk | Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Date: 2026-12-08T16:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails (EXEC DECISION)

Ben / Lauren / Elena —

Thanks for the analysis, Lauren, and for surfacing the ask, Ben. Exec decision below — I'm approving the deal to keep momentum but with specific, enforceable guardrails:

1) Discount: approve a 30% discount on the inference hosting line-item for Year 1 only. Discounts do not apply to professional services or add-on modules unless separately approved.

2) MFN: approve a narrowly scoped MFN limited to direct list-price parity for comparable published unit pricing, effective for 12 months from signature. Explicitly exclude marketplace/reseller programs, promotional campaign discounts, and partner-level rebates. Any benchmark/performance-based claims must be validated against Redwood's benchmark harness (we will provide the test spec).

3) Exclusivity: approve limited exclusivity covering two named SKUs (to be enumerated in the SOW) for 9 months. No blanket category or named-customer restrictions. All exclusivity language must include performance exit clauses and a defined SLA (availability + latency targets) that FinMetric can test against.

4) Risk controls: cap SLA credits at 10% of the annual contract value; require Legal to include an MFN sunset and audit rights limited to published receipts. Finance to confirm revenue recognition and quota treatment.

Lauren — proceed to incorporate these changes into the term sheet and send a redline to FinMetric. Ben — share the updated term sheet and the benchmark test spec when ready; ask FinMetric to confirm acceptance by EOD Wednesday to preserve the procurement cadence.

Marcus Lin
VP Sales | Redwood Inference

title: Trial hosting pathway: recap, recommended environment, and owners
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alice Park <alice@heliocare.ai>, Sam Rodriguez <sam.rodriguez@mediq-solutions.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 07 Dec 2026 09:18:00 -0800
Subject: Trial hosting pathway: recap, recommended environment, and owners

Hi Alice / Sam —

Great to speak this morning. Quick recap from our discovery and a proposed path for the 2-week trial so we can validate performance, cost, and integration effort before a production decision. I outlined the recommended environment options (hosted, dedicated sandbox, VPC trial), success criteria, and proposed owners below. Please confirm any constraints I missed and which environment you prefer to kick off.

Summary of ask from HelioCare call:
- Evaluate Redwood for real-time patient-summary generation in the HelioCare clinician UI (single-turn generation + tool calls to EHR retrieval)
- SLO target: p50 latency < 120ms for short prompts, p95 < 400ms (interactive UI requirement)
- Throughput: sustain ~40 concurrent users (roughly 60 requests/sec peak during rounds)
- Data residency: US-only; logs must not leave VPC for trial sensitive records
- Success metrics: latency targets, 95% pass on instruction-following tests (internal prompt set), cost projection for production scale (tokens/1M)

Environment options (short pros/cons):
1) Hosted API (fastest start)
   - Pros: no infra setup, start in hours, Redwood handles routing and scaling.
   - Cons: less control over token logging/residency; may not satisfy audit/PII guardrails for trial data without additional config.

2) Dedicated sandbox (recommended default)
   - Pros: dedicated GPU capacity with predictable latency, isolated instance for your team, control over model version and quantization profile, easier to produce stable perf numbers. Supports prefix/KV caching tuning to hit latency/cost targets.
   - Cons: needs short provisioning window (~24–48 hrs) and a small reserved commit for the trial window.

3) VPC trial (private deployment)
   - Pros: full control, logs and traffic stay in your VPC; meets strict compliance.
   - Cons: setup overhead for peering/iptables, longer lead time (3–7 business days), engineering lift on both sides.

Recommendation: start with a 2-week Dedicated sandbox sized for ~2–4 RTX-class GPUs (we’ll right-size after first 48 hours) and enable VPC peering for audit logs if you want logs to stay in your environment. If compliance blocks dedicated, we’ll move to a short VPC pilot. Hosted API is fallback for a quick functional proof but won’t give the deterministic latency numbers you asked for.

Proposed success criteria (concrete):
- Latency: p50 < 120ms, p95 < 400ms on representative clinician prompt set (1000 samples)
- Throughput: maintain 60 req/s sustained for 10 minutes without error rate > 1%
- Quality: instruction-following pass rate >= 95% on a labeled prompt set (200 prompts)
- Cost target: baseline token cost projection <= $X per 1M tokens (we will run synthetic pass to estimate)
- Security: trial environment must meet data residency / logging constraints (logs to customer VPC or masked)

Action owners & next steps
- Ben (me): provision Dedicated sandbox + initial model variant and quantization profile; provide pre-populated eval set and simple test harness (ETA: 24–48 hrs after signoff).
- Marissa: run onboarding session and shared test plan with HelioCare engineers; run evaluation with clinician prompts (ETA: schedule 2 business days after sandbox live).
- Jonas: lead VPC peering if HelioCare requires logs to remain in their network; coordinate network tests and firewall rules (ETA: within trial window if requested).
- Alice / Sam: confirm trial start date, share sample prompt set and any PII masking rules, and provide list of engineers to be added to the sandbox.

Attachments: trial-eval-matrix.xlsx (baseline prompts, test harness plan), vpc-onboarding-checklist.pdf

If that plan looks good I’ll start the provisioning once you confirm the start date.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com



From: Alice Park <alice@heliocare.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Rodriguez <sam.rodriguez@mediq-solutions.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 07 Dec 2026 10:43:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial hosting pathway: recap, recommended environment, and owners

Ben —

Thanks for the clear recap. That matches our internal notes. A couple of clarifications and one constraint:
- Data residency: for the trial we can send de-identified records, but logs with patient IDs need to stay inside our VPC for audit. We can accept a dedicated sandbox if you can support log shipping/mirroring into our VPC (or push masked logs to a customer bucket).
- Timeline: our clinical team prefers the trial to start the week of Dec 13 to align with clinician availability. Two weeks after that is ideal.
- Success metrics: add hallucination tolerance threshold: < 2% critical errors on diagnosis extraction tasks.

If Dedicated sandbox + log mirroring to our VPC is possible with that timeline, we’d like to go ahead. Please put Jonas on copy for peering logistics. I attached a short prompt sample and our masking rules (sent separately).

Cheers,
Alice Park
CTO, HelioCare
alice@heliocare.ai



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alice Park <alice@heliocare.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Sam Rodriguez <sam.rodriguez@mediq-solutions.com>
Date: Mon, 07 Dec 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial hosting pathway: recap, recommended environment, and owners

Perfect — Dec 13 start works. Quick notes and updated plan based on your constraints:

1) Dedicated sandbox + log mirroring
   - We’ll provision the Dedicated sandbox and enable a logging export to a customer-owned S3 bucket in the US region. If you prefer VPC peering for direct ingestion, Jonas will coordinate. Either approach will keep patient IDs from being stored in Redwood-managed logs.

2) Trial schedule
   - Dec 13: sandbox live + onboarding call (Marissa to run)
   - Dec 14–16: baseline perf runs + initial tuning (Ben / Jonas)
   - Dec 17–20: clinician sessions + quality evaluation (Marissa / HelioCare clinical leads)
   - Dec 21: checkpoint review and decision on next steps (extend to VPC trial or plan production rollout)

3) Updated success criteria (including hallucination metric)
   - Latency: p50 < 120ms, p95 < 400ms on 1k-sample set
   - Throughput: 60 req/s sustained, error rate < 1%
   - Quality: instruction-follow pass rate >= 95% and hallucination-critical-error rate < 2% on labeled set
   - Security: customer logs in customer-controlled bucket/VPC; no unmasked patient IDs stored in Redwood logs

4) Ownership & immediate actions
   - Ben: provision sandbox and upload our pre-populated eval harness; share access and short runbook (ETA: by end of Tue Dec 8).
   - Marissa: coordinate onboarding call and invite clinical participants (ETA: schedule by Wed Dec 9).
   - Jonas: confirm VPC peering steps and provide required CIDR/endpoint info (ETA: initial email by Tue Dec 8).
   - Alice / HelioCare: share sample prompt set + PII masking rules and list of engineers to be added.

I attached the trial-eval-matrix.xlsx with the baseline prompt categories. Jonas — can you confirm whether VPC peering is required up front or we should do S3 log export as the default?

Ben



From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Alice Park <alice@heliocare.ai>
Date: Tue, 08 Dec 2026 15:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial hosting pathway: recap, recommended environment, and owners

On Mon, Dec 7, 2026 at 2:05 PM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Jonas — can you confirm whether VPC peering is required up front or we should do S3 log export as the default?

Hi team —

S3 log export is the fastest path and meets the audit requirement if HelioCare controls the bucket and we set lifecycle/retention rules per their policy. I recommend we start with S3 export and keep VPC peering as option B if they insist on direct egress. Steps I’ll take this week:
- Send required CIDR and endpoint info to HelioCare (by EOD Tue Dec 8)
- Validate RBAC for the customer bucket and confirm encryption with their KMS (Wed Dec 9)
- Run a short connectivity test once sandbox is live (Dec 13)

If HelioCare confirms they need VPC peering from the start, we’ll move the timeline and I’ll schedule a kickoff with their network team. For the two-week trial window S3 export + masked payloads should be sufficient and faster.

Jonas Weber
Infrastructure Lead, Redwood Inference
jonas@redwood.com

From: Alyssa Gomez <alyssa@clearvector.com>

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. Attaching our completed vendor onboarding form (ClearVector_Onboard_Form.pdf). We need a provisional PO number to start billing for the initial integration work — expected first invoices mid-December. Our initial billing cadence will be monthly.

High-level:
- Requested monthly spend cap: $75,000 USD (we'll track against it).
- Two SKU lines for the integration phase: CV-INTEG-01 (professional services) and CV-SW-001 (platform usage).
- We sometimes send consolidated invoices with multiple lines; does Redwood accept a provisional PO that covers multiple invoice lines (yes/no)?

Also, our procurement team occasionally insists on a vendor-level spend cap and line-item mapping for compliance. If you can provide a provisional PO number and the PO header format you require on invoices that will speed us up.

Thanks — CCing our procurement inbox if they need to add details.

Best,
Alyssa Gomez
Head of Finance Operations
ClearVector, Inc.

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.example.com>

Date: Mon, 7 Dec 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Kickoff: ClearVector onboarding — PO numbers & spend caps

Thanks Alyssa — quick answers and next steps from our side.

1) Provisional PO: Procurement needs to issue a provisional (provisioning) PO so your first invoice can be approved. Priyom / Laura — can we reserve a provisional PO for ClearVector while they finalize invoice header? Suggested prefix: RV-ONB.

2) Spend cap: $75k/month is fine to start. Please confirm whether that cap applies to a calendar month or a 30-day rolling window.

3) Line items: We generally prefer line items to map to our internal cost centers. If you can provide a CSV with SKU -> description -> unit price -> currency, procurement can import it (see attached PO template RV_PO_Template.xlsx).

Subject: Re: Onboarding docs - VAT cert and next steps

Hi Camila,

We can confirm the legal name for invoices: Altanova Sp. z o.o.
Poland is our tax residency; we do not have a separate tax residency attestation beyond the VAT registration — we can provide a signed letter if required.

About invoice format: we can populate <TaxRegistrationNumber> in the XML with our NIP and add free-text lines for PO and billing entity.

Cc: Marta Zielinska <marta.zielinska@altanova.pl>, Piotr Nowak <p.nowak@altanova.pl>
Date: Mon, 7 Dec 2026 15:07:00 +01:00
Subject: RE: Onboarding docs - VAT cert and next steps

Hi all — tagging in finance guidance.

1) Billing entity: please invoice Redwood Inference EU B.V. (our Dutch subsidiary). That avoids US withholding and makes VAT handling simpler.
2) VAT treatment: since Altanova and Redwood EU are both in the EU, on B2B services the reverse-charge mechanism typically applies. Please do not charge Polish VAT on the invoice. Instead include: 
   - Vendor VAT ID (NIP)
   - Our EU VAT ID: NL123456789B01 (use this in the <CustomerTaxId> field)
   - A line on the invoice: \"Reverse charge — Article 44 Council Directive 2006/112/EC\" or similar wording.
3) PO: official PO number for the first purchase is PO-2026-6451. We've attached the signed PO. Please include the PO number in the header and in the XML <OrderReference> tag.

Attachments: PO-2026-6451.pdf

If Altanova needs a signed tax residency declaration for local bank/payment workflows, we can accept a signed PDF letter. Otherwise the VAT certificate + sample invoice is sufficient for us to process payment.

Ben Carter
Senior Manager, Accounts Payable
Redwood Inference
[attachment: PO-2026-6451.pdf (application/pdf)]

title: Follow-up: Dedicated vs Private VPC - ops playbook & next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Laura Gomez <laura@medixhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap: deployment isolation needs

Hi Laura — thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap and a few clarifying questions so we can scope a clean canary and SOW: 

- Your primary requirement: processing PHI from patient intake forms and clinician notes. You emphasized strict logical and network separation from other tenants.
- Preference expressed: Private VPC (customer-managed control plane) but you indicated Dedicated capacity could be acceptable if we can provide customer KMS/BYOK and documented egress controls.
- Questions we promised to follow up on:
  * Can you confirm whether any data will need to reside on-prem at day 1, or is on-prem only a future option?
  * Who owns the KMS key management decisions (security or infra team)?
  * Timeline constraints from procurement (target go-live window)?

Next steps I propose (feel free to edit):
1) We prepare an \"isolation proof\" checklist and a short risk matrix for your security team (we can get this to you within 3 business days).
2) Align on a canary window (2–3 day pilot) where we run real traffic into a Private VPC or Dedicated slice depending on your final choice.
3) Draft a narrow SOW covering canary, KMS integration, and acceptance criteria.

Attaching a small template risk matrix + initial SOW outline in my next message. If you can answer the three bullets above it will speed things up.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Sr. Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

[attachment: medix_risk_matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet)]

From: Laura Gomez <laura@medixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 11:03:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Post-call recap: deployment isolation needs

Hi Ben — appreciate the clear recap. Answers inline below: 

- Data classification: yes, PHI and PII. We'll need full HIPAA controls for processing and auditability.
- On-prem: not today. On-prem is a possibility in 12–18 months, but for the pilot and initial production we expect cloud hosting only.
- KMS ownership: security team owns policy; infra team will own key rotation ops. We will need BYOK support and the ability to audit key usage.
- Procurement timeline: target award by end of Q1 2027, but security review needs to see separation evidence before procurement signs.

If you can provide the checklist and a short operations playbook that shows how you enforce tenant separation and KMS integration, we can route that to our security panel this week.

Thanks —

preview:

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As discussed, procurement and security want to capture the BYO KMS onboarding details in a single spreadsheet so our compliance team can sign off quickly. I've attached a working template (acme_byo_onboarding_table_v1.xlsx) with the questions we need answered across: key import, key wrapping, attestation, rotation windows, and TLS endpoints.

High level asks:
- Can Redwood accept wrapped key blobs from our HSM vendor (Thales) and import into a customer-scoped KEK?
- Do you provide an HSM-backed key wrapping key (KWK) option or do customers manage the KWK in their own HSM?
- What's the guaranteed window from key activation to full cluster propagation?

Marta
Security Operations | Acme Corp

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marta Gomez <marta.gomez@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 11:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: BYO KMS onboarding spreadsheet & initial questions

Marta — thanks for the template and clear asks. Looping in Karthik (security/ops) and Laura (legal/contract) to own the technical and acceptance wording. We can turn around the completed worksheet and initial artifacts by EOD Friday (12/04).

Quick answers so you have immediate context:
- We support importing wrapped key material from FIPS-certified HSMs where the wrap uses an agreed KEK algorithm (we commonly handle Thales and HSM PKCS#11 export formats).
- Customers can choose: Redwood-hosted HSM KWK (managed, HSM-backed) or BYOK where the customer keeps KWK in their HSM and we accept wrapped DEKs.
- Typical propagation after activation is 1–3 minutes for KV cache sync plus up to 30 minutes for cross-region rotation depending on your topology; Karthik will add precise SLOs.

I'll follow up once Karthik has the document filled.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Marta Gomez <marta.gomez@acmecorp.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Dec 2026 15:26:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: BYO KMS onboarding spreadsheet & initial questions

Marta — attached is the completed worksheet and a short ops pack (redwood-byo-ops-answers.pdf) plus a TLS cipher summary (tls-ciphers-summary.txt). Key points from the technical review: 

1) Import/wrapped keys
- We accept vendor-wrapped DEKs if the wrap/unwrapping algorithm is documented and FIPS 140-2/140-3 compliant (we've onboarded Thales and Utimaco customers).
- Supported envelope formats: PKCS#7, CMS with RSA-KEM, and vendor-specific wrapped blob formats (we map at onboarding).

2) KWK choices
- Managed KWK: Redwood provisions an HSM-backed KWK in our FIPS-validated HSM cluster; we manage lifecycle and key rotation as per the managed-KWK SLA.
- Customer KWK (BYOK): Customer holds KWK in their HSM or cloud KMS and provides wrapped DEKs for import; in this mode we never have unwrapped KWK and logs show only wrapped material handling.

3) Rotation and propagation
- Rolling rotation within a single region: active -> standby cutover completes within 3 minutes (typical).
- Global fleet rotation (multi-region): staged rollout, default 30 minute window for full KV propagation; can be tightened to 10–15 minutes with dedicated reserved capacity and pre-warmed cache policies.

4) Key escrows & export
- We do not export private key material from customer-managed HSMs. For managed-KWK customers, we offer a sealed-escrow option with dual control (two-person) for emergency recovery; details can be included in the contract annex.

5) Audit & attestation
- We can provide HSM attestation reports and a signed statement of key handling practices. SOC/Security artifacts are provided via a secure link or NDA.

6) TLS endpoints
- All control and inter-node traffic use mTLS (mutual TLS) with strong cipher suites (see tls-ciphers-summary.txt). Public API endpoints terminate on our edge LB with TLS 1.3; we support TLS 1.2 (modern) on request for legacy integrations.

See attachments for table row-by-row answers and recommended acceptance language. If you want, we can provide a short runbook for key import testing with your Thales team.

-Karthik
Senior Security Engineer | Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-byo-ops-answers.pdf (8 pages), tls-ciphers-summary.txt (1KB)

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Marta Gomez <marta.gomez@acmecorp.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 6 Dec 2026 09:47:00 -08:00

I've attached a working template (acme_byo_onboarding_table_v1.xlsx) with the questions we need answered across: key import, key wrapping, attestation, rotation windows, and TLS endpoints.

High level asks:
- Can Redwood accept wrapped key blobs from our HSM vendor (Thales) and import into a customer-scoped KEK?
- Do you provide an HSM-backed key wrapping key (KWK) option or do customers manage the KWK in their own HSM?

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marta Gomez <marta.gomez@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 11:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: BYO KMS onboarding spreadsheet & initial questions

Marta — thanks for the template and clear asks. Looping in Karthik (security/ops) and Laura (legal/contract) to own the technical and acceptance wording. We can turn around the completed worksheet and initial artifacts by EOD Friday (12/04).

Quick answers so you have immediate context:
- We support importing wrapped key material from FIPS-certified HSMs where the wrap uses an agreed KEK algorithm (we commonly handle Thales and HSM PKCS#11 export formats).
- Customers can choose: Redwood-hosted HSM KWK (managed, HSM-backed) or BYOK where the customer keeps KWK in their HSM and we accept wrapped DEKs.
- Typical propagation after activation is 1–3 minutes for KV cache sync plus up to 30 minutes for cross-region rotation depending on your topology; Karthik will add precise SLOs.

I'll follow up once Karthik has the document filled.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Marta Gomez <marta.gomez@acmecorp.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Dec 2026 15:26:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: BYO KMS onboarding spreadsheet & initial questions

Marta — attached is the completed worksheet and a short ops pack (redwood-byo-ops-answers.pdf) plus a TLS cipher summary (tls-ciphers-summary.txt). Key points from the technical review: 

1) Import/wrapped keys
- We accept vendor-wrapped DEKs if the wrap/unwrapping algorithm is documented and FIPS 140-2/140-3 compliant (we've onboarded Thales and Utimaco customers).
- Supported envelope formats: PKCS#7, CMS with RSA-KEM, and vendor-specific wrapped blob formats (we map at onboarding).

2) KWK choices
- Managed KWK: Redwood provisions an HSM-backed KWK in our FIPS-validated HSM cluster; we manage lifecycle and key rotation as per the managed-KWK SLA.
- Customer KWK (BYOK): Customer holds KWK in their HSM or cloud KMS and provides wrapped DEKs for import; in this mode we never have unwrapped KWK and logs show only wrapped material handling.

3) Rotation and propagation
- Rolling rotation within a single region: active -> standby cutover completes within 3 minutes (typical).
- Global fleet rotation (multi-region): staged rollout, default 30 minute window for full KV propagation; can be tightened to 10–15 minutes with dedicated reserved capacity and pre-warmed cache policies.

4) Key escrows & export
- We do not export private key material from customer-managed HSMs. For managed-KWK customers, we offer a sealed-escrow option with dual control (two-person) for emergency recovery; details can be included in the contract annex.

5) Audit & attestation
- We can provide HSM attestation reports and a signed statement of key handling practices. SOC/Security artifacts are provided via a secure link or NDA.

6) TLS endpoints
- All control and inter-node traffic use mTLS (mutual TLS) with strong cipher suites (see tls-ciphers-summary.txt). Public API endpoints terminate on our edge LB with TLS 1.3; we support TLS 1.2 (modern) on request for legacy integrations.

See attachments for table row-by-row answers and recommended acceptance language. If you want, we can provide a short runbook for key import testing with your Thales team.

-Karthik
Senior Security Engineer | Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-byo-ops-answers.pdf (8 pages), tls-ciphers-summary.txt (1KB)

title: Bridgecrest - request for bespoke p95 latency carveout and ops playbook
preview:
From: Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Maya Reddy <maya.reddy@bridgecrest.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 15:08:00 -0800
Subject: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the model outputs look promising. We have a narrow window to launch a latency-sensitive feature (real-time search suggestion) that requires a stronger p95 commitment than your standard SLA. Specifically:

- Target: p95 <= 100ms for text-generation inference at 512 tokens, sustained on expected QPS (burst to 300 TPS).
- Duration: initial 6-month commitment for the pilot, with a potential expansion if performance holds.

Our procurement team has asked for a written carveout or addendum that codifies this for the pilot (performance metric + operational runbook + rollback triggers). I attached a short PDF with our ask and estimated traffic patterns (Bridgecrest_SLA_Request_20261201.pdf).

Can Redwood support this via a dedicated pool / capacity reservation, or would you recommend a variant/fallback approach? We'd like to resolve commercial terms quickly — procurement will need a target DocuSign by Dec 15 to hit our internal launch.

Appreciate the quick guidance.

— Alex
Alex Cho
Head of Platform Partnerships
Bridgecrest.ai



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>, Bridgecrest Procurement <procurement@bridgecrest.ai>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 09:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

Thanks Alex — looping in infra and product to assess feasibility and the expected operational impact. Marcus / Grace: can you please review the attached ask and provide:

- A feasibility assessment (what we can meet today vs what requires dedicated capacity)
- An estimated incremental cost / RU for a dedicated pool to hit p95 <= 100ms at Bridgecrest's traffic profile
- Required runbook items (rollback triggers, throttles, fallbacks) we should include in the addendum

Proposed internal timeline:
- Dec 3: quick tech estimate from product/infra
- Dec 6: draft runbook + commercial ask for legal/procurement
- Dec 8–10: exec review (if needed)

CC'd Vivek in case we need SLA governance sign-off / escalation path.

Thanks — Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Dec 2026 11:27:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

Quick technical readout from infra: short answer — possible but conditional. Notes below. Attachment: latency-ops-estimates.csv (stub).

Baseline: our hosted p95 for comparable 512-token workloads on the current L2 family is ~140–180ms across multi-tenant pools under 99th percentile QPS.

Options to reach p95<=100ms for the pilot:

1) Dedicated GPU pool (preferred)
   - Pros: predictable tail latency, isolate noisy neighbors, we can tune batch sizes and kernel selection per-model.
   - Cons: cost jump (estimated +40–60% RU for reserved capacity at projected load).

2) Intelligent routing + KV prefix caching
   - Pros: lower incremental spend, best for repeat prefix patterns.
   - Cons: only helps if Bridgecrest traffic has high prefix locality.

3) Model variant (smaller but tuned) as fallback
   - Pros: graceful degradation with guaranteed lower latency; preserves uptime if dedicated pool saturates.

Operational asks if we proceed with dedicated pool:
- Canary deploy + 72-hour monitoring window (p95 telemetry in Datadog dashboard)
- Auto-fallback policy to L2 model with increased per-request guardrails when p95 crosses threshold
- Runbook sections: incident playbook, escalation matrix (include Redwood SLA governance contact), capacity scaling steps, contact list for Bridgecrest on-call

I'll attach the latency estimate spreadsheet. Need product to confirm which model family Bridgecrest wants to pin and whether they'll accept a fallback variant in the addendum.

— Marcus
Marcus Lin
Principal Infra Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>, Bridgecrest Procurement <procurement@bridgecrest.ai>
Date: Sat, 5 Dec 2026 09:15:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

High-level governance note: Redwood can agree to a pilot-level carveout with conditions. Operational and legal prerequisites we need before any SLA promise:

- Signed addendum that defines the metric (exact inference config, tokenization, model version, dataset for test harness)
- Agreed rollback and incident thresholds (we should use metric-based guards, e.g., consecutive 5-minute windows where p95 > 120% of target)
- An ops-runbook itemizing who executes scaling steps and the time-to-scale expectations
- Commercial cliff: pilot pricing and a clear uplift for dedicated capacity; procurement must accept a capacity reservation with a minimum commitment for the pilot period

Product ask: Grace, can you draft the runbook skeleton (3–4 page) and include a short FAQ for procurement. Marcus: keep the latency spreadsheet ready for the commercial deck. Aisha: I will take point on the SLA governance review and prepare a one-slide exec summary if we need C-level sign-off.

I've uploaded a first-pass runbook draft to the internal drive and attached a markdown copy for review (redwood-ops-runbook-draft.md).

— Vivek
Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>, Bridgecrest Procurement <procurement@bridgecrest.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Dec 2026 11:24:00 -08:00
Subject: Bridgecrest pilot path and next steps (addendum + runbook)

Alex / Maya / Procurement team — thanks for the patience and the clear ask. Short summary of what Redwood can commit for the 6-month pilot:

Agreed pilot approach:
- Dedicated reserved GPU pool sized for Bridgecrest's expected QPS (Marcus to provide precise RU delta).
- Measured p95 target: <= 100ms for the agreed inference config (model X, max tokens 512) with the following caveats:
  - Commitment is for the pilot window and contingent on Bridgecrest accepting the operational runbook and fallback policy.
  - Fallback: automatic routing to a tuned L2 variant if p95 breaches the threshold during sustained windows; this is explicitly allowed in the addendum.

Operational and commercial asks (action items):
- Bridgecrest: review and sign the addendum (DocuSign link below) by Dec 15 to keep the launch schedule. The addendum will reference the runbook and the model pin.
- Redwood (Marcus / Grace): finalize RU estimates and runbook draft by EOD Dec 6 (Grace to lead runbook).
- Redwood (Vivek): SLA governance will perform a final review upon runbook submission; will notify if exec sign-off is needed.

Links / docs:
- Addendum (DocuSign): https://www.docusign.com/tx/BRG-4521-addendum
- Internal runbook draft: https://redwood.atlassian.net/l/c/BRG-4521-runbook
- HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/BRG-4521

Next sync: propose Dec 8 at 10am PT to align on commercial terms and confirm sign-off owners. Please confirm availability.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

1) PO mapping: PO-45271 is the correct purchase order for initial services and should be present on all invoices. We tag invoices in the payops system by PO number in the invoice header (field: \"PO_NUMBER\"). For automated reconciliation we expect the remit string to include: PO_NUMBER|INVOICE_NUMBER|VENDOR_ID (example: 45271|INV-20261201-03|PL-9982).

2) Micro-deposit: We do micro-deposits for ACH verification. If you approve, finance will initiate two small deposits tomorrow morning in UTC and notify you when posted. Please reply with the exact account nickname on your side to confirm.

3) Wire fallback: For wires, we require the bank letter (you provided it), plus beneficiary name and an ABW/beneficiary reference in the wire memo that includes the PO number.

Next steps: 
- Jordan: confirm account nickname for micro-deposit and whether you want ACH only or ACH + wire fallback.
- Priyom: can you take payops tasks once micro-deposits come in? I CC'd Marissa for PO/portal confirmation if any portal changes are required.

Ben

title: Customer DPA redline: telemetry & subprocessor clarifications needed
preview:
From: Claire Dawson <claire@lumenai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Hall <m.hall@lumenai.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T09:18:00-08:00
Subject: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts

Hi Ben,

Thanks for sending the DPA draft. Markus and I scanned the changes; we attached our redline (LumenAI_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf). A couple of points we need clarified before legal proceeds:

- Telemetry: your team’s proposed definitions use "operational telemetry" vs "analytic telemetry." We need to understand what fields are included under each bucket and the retention windows. Our security policy prohibits retention of customer prompt/response pairs beyond 30 days without explicit waiver.
- Subprocessors: the subprocessor_matrix.csv only lists generic roles. Can you confirm named vendors for telemetry/metrics, usage analytics, and model hosting? Do you require any customer data for model improvement or training?
- Training/data use carveout: the redline currently has a broad clause allowing "aggregate" model improvement. We need specifics on whether prompt/response content could be used to fine-tune models and whether LumenAI can opt-out per workspace.

We're trying to keep this moving — can you escalate internally to Security/Product/Legal so we can get a definitive yes/no on: (1) telemetry retention ceiling, (2) explicit non-training guarantee or opt-out flow, and (3) named subprocessors for telemetry/analytics?

Thanks,
Claire

--
Claire Dawson
Head of Contracts, LumenAI
claire@lumenai.com
+1 415-555-1212


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Claire Dawson <claire@lumenai.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts

Claire — thanks for the redline and the clear asks. I've looped in our Legal (Olga), Privacy (Priya) and Product (Karthik) internally to get firm answers. Quick notes for you while we triage:

- Telemetry fields: by default we collect event-level metrics (latency, error rates), token-level counts, and anonymized usage histograms. We do not persist full prompt/response pairs by default in the hosted product; when customers enable debug logs we retain request/response content for troubleshooting.
- Retention: our standard hosted retention is 90 days for operational telemetry, 365 days for aggregated metrics. We understand this may be longer than LumenAI’s policy; we can propose a contract carveout to 30 days if required for the account but need Product/Security signoff on the operational impact.
- Training: Redwood does not train models on customer prompt/response data by default. We can include explicit contractual language that customer content will not be used for model training unless customer gives an opt-in.

I'll get back to you with approvals and suggested contract language by EOD. Attaching the files you sent into our thread for internal review.

Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0199


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-12-06T11:04:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts — need signoff

Team — forwarding LumenAI's redline and their questions (attached). Key asks summarized below:

1) Telemetry: they want ability to limit retention to 30 days for prompt/response content and detailed field-level definition of telemetry buckets. Our standard: 90d operational content, 365d aggregated metrics.
2) Subprocessors: they want named vendors for telemetry/analytics/model hosting. We currently list categories; they need named vendors for audit.
3) Training: they require explicit contractual non-training of customer content (opt-in model if we want to use data).

Impact/ask:
- Product: confirm whether we can support a 30-day retention option per customer on hosted Redwood and any runtime/observability features that would be impacted (debug logs, traceability). Also need to know if "no-training" guarantees are feasible without engineering changes.
- Privacy/Security: provide recommended contractual language for the non-training guarantee, and confirm whether any subprocessors need to be redacted or annotated (e.g., Observair for metrics, ModelHub for hosted model caching).
- Legal (Olga): if you can propose redlines we should push back with, ideal language for both opt-out and opt-in flows, and whether an exception for ephemeral debugging session storage is acceptable.

Attachments: LumenAI_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf, subprocessor_matrix.csv (from customer).

Target: I'd like to send a consolidated response + proposed contract text back to Claire by 3pm PST. Can you confirm availability to review/approve by 2pm?

Thanks — Ben


From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T13:06:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts — need signoff

Ben / all — quick internal response after syncing with Privacy/Security:

1) Telemetry retention
- Legal is comfortable with offering a contract option for a 30-day retention ceiling for request/response content on hosted accounts, provided Product confirms operational feasibility. Proposed clause: "Redwood will, upon customer request, configure hosted account telemetry retention for request and response content to a maximum of 30 days. Operational telemetry and aggregated metrics may be retained longer as anonymized aggregates."
- We should carve out explicit language for debug logs: "Debug logs containing full request/response content may be retained for up to 90 days for troubleshooting when enabled by the customer; such logs will be accessible only to authorized personnel under documented access controls."

2) Named subprocessors
- Security confirms the following named vendors may be disclosed: Observair (metrics), ModelCache Inc. (model caching), CloudRun GPUs LLC (managed hosting). We will provide the subprocessor CSV with names and contact points. Note: some vendor agreements contain confidentiality clauses — we must not include vendor contract rates or internal terms.

3) Training/data use
- Privacy recommends the following binding language: "Redwood will not use Customer Content to train, improve, or retrain models absent the Customer's explicit, written opt-in. Aggregated, de-identified metrics that do not contain Customer Content may be used for product analytics and performance tuning."
- Product note: need confirmation that feature flags exist to ensure no training pipeline consumes customer content by default.

Operational impact & decision path:
- Priya: please confirm whether the opt-out/opt-in control is implementable within the current privacy controls; if not, we should include a technical milestone in the contract to deliver the control within X days post-signature.
- Karthik: confirm whether a per-customer retention override to 30d is feasible and any perf implications for debugging.

If Product/Security agree, Legal can prepare a proposed DPA amendment and redline to send back to LumenAI.

Olga

--
Olga Petrov
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwood.com


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T14:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts — need signoff

Quick confirmation from Privacy:
- Non-training guarantee: approved. We can contractually guarantee "no training" without immediate engineering changes by ensuring data never flows into model-training pipelines for hosted customers by default. We'll add a delivery milestone for a UI-based opt-in for customers who want to contribute data; timing TBD (recommend 60 days after signature).

Hi Ben,

Thanks for sending the DPA draft. Markus and I scanned the changes; we attached our redline (LumenAI_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf). A couple of points we need clarified before legal proceeds:

- Telemetry: your team’s proposed definitions use "operational telemetry" vs "analytic telemetry." We need to understand what fields are included under each bucket and the retention windows. Our security policy prohibits retention of customer prompt/response pairs beyond 30 days without explicit waiver.
- Subprocessors: the subprocessor_matrix.csv only lists generic roles. Can you confirm named vendors for telemetry/metrics, usage analytics, and model hosting? Do you require any customer data for model improvement or training?
- Training/data use carveout: the redline currently has a broad clause allowing "aggregate" model improvement. We need specifics on whether prompt/response content could be used to fine-tune models and whether LumenAI can opt-out per workspace.

We're trying to keep this moving — can you escalate internally to Security/Product/Legal so we can get a definitive yes/no on: (1) telemetry retention ceiling, (2) explicit non-training guarantee or opt-out flow, and (3) named subprocessors for telemetry/analytics?

Thanks,
Claire

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>

Date: 2026-12-06T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts

Claire — thanks for the redline and the clear asks. I've looped in our Legal (Olga), Privacy (Priya) and Product (Karthik) internally to get firm answers. Quick notes for you while we triage:

- Telemetry fields: by default we collect event-level metrics (latency, error rates), token-level counts, and anonymized usage histograms. We do not persist full prompt/response pairs by default in the hosted product; when customers enable debug logs we retain request/response content for troubleshooting.
- Retention: our standard hosted retention is 90 days for operational telemetry, 365 days for aggregated metrics. We understand this may be longer than LumenAI’s policy; we can propose a contract carveout to 30 days if required for the account but need Product/Security signoff on the operational impact.
- Training: Redwood does not train models on customer prompt/response data by default. We can include explicit contractual language that customer content will not be used for model training unless customer gives an opt-in.

I'll get back to you with approvals and suggested contract language by EOD. Attaching the files you sent into our thread for internal review.

Ben

title: ShopFront checkout: urgent throughput & p99 spike
preview:
From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: support@shopfront.com
Date: 2026-12-05T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Hi Ava,

We're seeing a significant degradation in checkout performance this morning during our peak window (08:45–09:15 PST). Key symptoms: 
- p95 steady but p99 jumped from ~450ms to ~2.8s starting ~08:50.
- error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on checkout API for ~15 minutes.
- throughput (requests/sec) held roughly equal — not a traffic surge.

This is impacting conversions on the storefront. I've attached traces and a small set of sampled p99 payloads. Can you triage and advise whether this is on your side (model serving) or ours (request patterns)?

Attachments: [shopfront-checkout-traces.zip (1.2MB)], [p99-samples.txt]

Thanks — need a quick heads-up to share with our ops team.

— Liam
Head of Platform, ShopFront
liam.oneill@shopfront.com


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-05T09:26:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Marcus / Rishi — forwarding an urgent report from ShopFront (Deal SF-4721). They see p99 spikes and 5xxs during their checkout peak; traffic didn't jump. Attached traces from customer. Can you: 
1) Triage root cause (queueing/backpressure/concurrency)
2) Apply short-term mitigations we can push in the next 15–30m
3) Draft a <5 min customer update I can send to Liam

Evelyn I cc'd for visibility — might need exec update if this persists.

Forwarded message:
> From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
> Date: 2026-12-05 09:12 PST
> Subject: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent
> 
> [original body and attachments]

— Ava
Account Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-05T09:34:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Liam — thanks for the data and flagging this. We're on it. Quick update for your ops: 
- We've started a limited mitigation: enabling an immediate fallback to the smaller model variant (redwood-lite-1b) for checkout route to reduce per-request CPU.
- We've also temporarily set a conservative concurrency cap for your account route to 50 concurrent requests to ease queueing.
- SRE is investigating whether backpressure from long-context KV cache churn caused worker stalls.

Expect a follow-up in ~30m with concrete metrics. If the mitigations reduce errors, we'll propose a short credit for the affected window while we complete the postmortem.

Action items I own: confirm fallbacks applied, monitor p99/p50/p95 and 5xx, propose credit amount.

— Marcus
Senior Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
Date: 2026-12-05T09:56:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Marcus/Ava — quick technical summary and what we changed (executed at 09:40 PST):
Findings (initial):
- Root cause appears to be KV cache churn on long sequences for a subset of checkout requests; those requests incur heavy KV cache recompute and push worker threads into long-tail processing.
- Concurrency cap for ShopFront route was set higher than intended in a config merge last week (cap=250 vs intended 75). During a steady request rate this allowed many requests to queue behind long-running ops, inflating p99.
- Backpressure from workers wasn't being propagated cleanly to the edge router due to an internal circuit tolerance mismatch.

Immediate mitigations applied:
- Reduced concurrency cap to 50 (config pushed at 09:40).
- Enabled aggressive prefix/KV caching for the checkout route to reduce recompute for repeated context.
- Activated fallback routing to redwood-lite-1b for very long contexts (threshold 2k tokens).
- Throttled large-context non-checkout requests for 30m.

Result so far: p99 has dropped from the 2.8s peak down to ~620ms within 12 minutes; 5xx rate down to 0.9%. Monitoring continues.

Next steps (post-incident):
- Root-cause postmortem and fix for the config drift that set the cap to 250.
- Improve backpressure propagation in edge router (SRE epic).
- Add an automated alert when route cap diverges from policy.

Links: metrics dashboard: https://monitoring.redwood.ai/d/shopsf/SF-4721-latency

I'll draft the technical notes for the postmortem — Marcus/Ava please let me know wording for customer update.

— Rishi
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-05T17:44:00-08:00
Subject: Exec note — ShopFront incident and customer communication

Team — thanks for the fast containment and the clear runbook. A few asks before we close the loop with the customer tomorrow morning: 
1) Customer update: Marcus, send a concise update (what happened, what we changed, current impact) and an estimated timeline for postmortem. Keep it <200 words.
2) Credits: I pre-approved a goodwill credit up to 10% for the billing period pending Marcus's recommendation.
3) Postmortem owners: Rishi please lead the RCA; include a timeline and the config-drift remediation plan.
4) Comms: I'll notify Sales leadership and CS once the final text is ready.

Let's finalize the customer note by 09:00 PST tomorrow so ShopFront can communicate internally.

— Evelyn
VP, Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference

title: Standby→Warmup gap causing p95/p99 latency spikes
preview:
From: Ella James <ella.james@fintechco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: FintechCo Platform <platform@fintechco.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: sudden p99 spikes during batch runway (first seen 08:50 PST)

Hi Ben,

We're seeing a sharp increase in p95/p99 latency for the Redwood hosted endpoint across our peak batch windows starting ~08:50 PST. p95 jumped from ~210ms to 1.4s and p99 is hitting 3.6s. This started after a surge where new GPU instances were brought up. The customer-facing jobs are timing out intermittently (2% errors), and this is impacting reconciliation jobs.

Attached is a short trace bundle and the CloudFront logs (fintechco_trace_12052026.zip). Can you confirm whether this aligns with a scaling warm-up problem on your side? We need immediate mitigation and a playbook — our ops lead is standing by.

Thanks,
Ella
Ella James
Platform Reliability Lead, FintechCo



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ella James <ella.james@fintechco.com>

Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 09:24:00 -08:00

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 09:42:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: sudden p99 spikes during batch runway (first seen 08:50 PST)

Ben / team — quick initial findings from infra telemetry:
- Autoscaler did trigger a standby->warm transition for FintechCo pool at 08:47:33 PST (scale activity id=scale-7784).
- Instance boot times: median 38s, but 12% of instances took 2.1–3.8 minutes due to a delayed NVMe firmware init on the current image (kernel 5.17 + vendor NVMe driver v2.4).
- When the pool is in the 'initializing' phase, tokenization and KV cache warming are deferred — requests are served by cold model processes that pay heavier attention memory setup, causing head latency to spike (seen in our trace).
- p95/p99 correlated to the proportion of requests routed to these slow nodes during the warmup window.

Action items I’m executing now:
1) Pulling the failing instance ids and marking them for forensic logs (collector running now).
2) Temporarily increasing routing weight to warm nodes with already-cached KV (to reduce surface area).
3) Rolling a short-lived config: lower max-boot-concurrency for the pool to avoid simultaneous NVMe init storms.

I’ll post updated telemetry in 20 minutes.

—Marcus

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Ella James <ella.james@fintechco.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 11:04:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: sudden p99 spikes during batch runway (first seen 08:50 PST)

Update after running targeted queries against traces and the init logs:
- Confirmed NVMe init delay on image img-v5.17-202611 — kernel messages show repeated controller resets that line up with the 2–4 minute boots.
- When NVMe init stalls, the model process stays in a partial-start state: grpc thread accepts connection but backend tokenization / kv warm path is delayed → inflated head times and occasional 503s when request queue fills.
- Workaround applied at 10:45 PST: set routing to favor already-initialized shards and increase retry-window for user jobs. This reduced p95 from 1.4s → 320ms and p99 from 3.6s → 780ms on sampled traffic. Error rate dropped from 2% → 0.4%. (See sla-impact-summary.pdf attached.)

Request: Marcus, can infra schedule a conservative image rollback or NVMe firmware pin for the FintechCo pool and a constrained boot-concurrency change (<=2) so we don't re-trigger a bootstorm during their next batch window?

Ella — Naomi will follow up on customer comms.

—Eve
Evelyn Hart
Platform Reliability Engineer, Redwood Inference



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ella James <ella.james@fintechco.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 17:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Post-mortem summary & next steps — standby->warm gap (INC-2026-4512)

Ella / team — quick summary and next steps. Looping Rishi for visibility.

What happened:
- During scale-up at ~08:47 PST, a subset of GPU instances experienced prolonged NVMe controller initialization on image img-v5.17-202611. Instances taking 2–4 minutes to finish init redirected traffic to partially-initialized model processes, inflating p95/p99 and causing a ~2% error rate in recon jobs.

Immediate mitigations performed today:
- Routing weight change to prefer warmed shards (applied 10:45 PST) — measurable latency improvement (p95 and p99 reduced).
- Short-term boot-concurrency cap applied to the pool to avoid parallel NVMe init storms.
- Forensic logs collected and trace bundle uploaded (fintechco_trace_12052026.zip).

Proposed next steps (owner / ETA):
- Marcus: schedule image rollback to v5.16 kernel and pin NVMe firmware for FintechCo pools (ETA: 48 hrs).
- Evelyn: add an init-health probe and fail-open behavior so partially-initialized processes do not accept external requests (ETA: 72 hrs).
- Naomi: propose customer-facing note + credit offer for impacted window and coordinate with Ella (ETA: 24 hrs).
- Ben / Rishi: executive summary to FintechCo execs and internal leadership (ETA: EOD Monday).

We’ll treat this as a priority post-mortem and add a specific warmup-runbook to the hosted API playbooks to prevent regression across other customers.

If you need anything else right now, call me directly.

—Ben
