Marissa / Kim — forwarding Elena's asks so we can lock configuration: portal push + email copy, consolidated monthly PDF + e-invoice XML, PO GF-9876 mapping, VAT cert attached.

Questions for ops:
- Marissa: can Billing Ops produce both a consolidated monthly PDF and an XML for the same invoice run? Any constraints on filename length/timestamp?
- Marissa: does our portal push include a VAT tag field that the customer can consume, or do we need to embed VAT metadata in the XML only?
- Kim: will consolidated monthly invoicing affect revenue recognition or billing cadence we need to flag?

Time pressure: client wants to be live week of Dec 7 — we need a short tech handoff and the SFTP/API credentials by Dec 4.

Thanks — Ben

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.example>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.example>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-12-02T13:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Internal: Brightline AP preference / PO GF-9876 — need configuration confirm

We can do consolidated monthly PDF + e-invoice XML via SFTP. Quick details:
- PDF: single file per legal entity, named BRIGHTLINE_YYYYMM_INVOICE.pdf (we can change naming on request).
- XML: UBL-based snapshot matching our sample schema (attached).

- PO mapping: by default we'll map the invoice header to the PO GF-9876 for this rollout. If they need line-level PO mapping we can add it but will need mapping rules from procurement.
- Filename limits: keep under 128 chars — no special characters.

I'll create the SFTP account + onboarding creds and share by Dec 3 EOD, and I'll schedule a 30-min handoff with their AP tech contact.

title: NovusAI dedicated migration: sizing validation sprint & contingency cadence
preview:
From: Nora Patel <nora@novusai.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Sizing ask for hosted→dedicated migration

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the product walkthrough last week. We just landed board approval to proceed with a Dedicated deployment for NovusAI's inference workloads and need to lock down sizing and a short validation/cutover window.

High level:
- Current hosted baseline: ~3.2M tokens/day, peak sustained qps ~1.8k for our chat endpoint (mostly 1–2k token responses).
- We expect seasonal spikes up to ~3.5k qps for two hour windows (Black Friday-ish).
- Required uptime SLA during business hours (PST): 99.95% to start.

Ask: Can you provide a concrete validation plan (traffic replay / soak), a recommended GPU fleet mix, and a contingency/rollback cadence for the actual cutover? We aim to do a 48–72h validation run in early Dec and cutover the week after if things look green.

I've looped in procurement (procurement@novusai.com) for any contract-side questions.

Thanks,
Nora Patel
Head of ML Platform
NovusAI


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Nora Patel <nora@novusai.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T11:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Sizing ask for hosted→dedicated migration

Nora —

Great news on the board approval, congrats. I've attached a quick extraction of your hosted usage (hosted-usage-export.csv) and we can use that to seed the validation.

Proposed immediate next steps:
- Ben to draft an initial capacity matrix and bench runs (we'll include quantized variants).
- Agree on a 48h replay window (we're flexible to do Dec 4–6 or Dec 7–9) — you pick.
- Karthik will propose acceptance gates and rollback criteria.
- We'll prepare a lightweight cutover-runbook and a hot rollback plan that can be executed within the 1-hour window.

I'll coordinate calendar invites once we agree dates.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Nora Patel <nora@novusai.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-30T10:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Sizing ask for hosted→dedicated migration

Nora/Ava —

Based on the hosted export, preliminary sizing suggestions:
- Baseline fleet: 6x A10G (or equivalent T4x2) worker nodes (8 GPUs total via multi-GPU pods) with model shard + KV cache enabled to hit the 1.8k qps baseline with p95 latency ~120–180ms for 2k-token responses.
- Buffer for spikes: design to absorb +2.0x concurrency for short bursts (autoscale up to 12x A10G equivalent).
- Cost/path trade: recommend keeping a quantized 4-bit variant for non-critical routes — saves ~40% GPU cycles during peak.

Validation plan (48h replay):
1) Stage mirror: replay 72h of production traffic at 1.0x velocity for sanity.
2) Soak test: 24h at 1.5x baseline (sustained) to confirm thermal and throughput characteristics.
3) Spike drills: automated bursts to 2.0x for 15–30min to validate autoscaling and fallback to hosted API if node-level issues occur.

Acceptance gates (short list): p95 latency <250ms, error rate <0.1%, KV hit rate >85%, no OOM events for 48h.

Attachments: capacity_matrix_v1.xlsx (I included per-route recommended GPU mapping) and inference-benchmarks.pdf with kernel-level numbers.

Operational notes: make sure your tokenizers are warmed (prefix cache) and prefer batched requests for small messages. We'll script client-side burst-smoothing if you want.

--
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Nora Patel <nora@novusai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: NovusAI Procurement <procurement@novusai.com>
Date: 2026-12-01T08:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Sizing ask for hosted→dedicated migration

Thanks Ben — this is helpful. Dec 4–6 works for the 48h replay + soak (we can start at 00:00 PST on Dec 4).

Two follow-ups:
1) For rollback, how long to fail back to hosted with zero customer impact? Any database switchover we should be aware of?
2) Will you be able to run the spike drills during our business hours (we'd like to validate autoscale and response during PST peak at 11:00–13:00 PST).

I'll ask procurement to review the MA and get the signer in place — expect an update by EOD tomorrow.

Nora


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Nora Patel <nora@novusai.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-01T11:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Sizing ask for hosted→dedicated migration

Nora —

Quick answers and details for the runbook:
Rollback timing and impact:
- Hot rollback to hosted: target 20–45 minutes end-to-end for DNS cutover + client-side retry window, assuming the hosted endpoints are warm. We'll keep an active warmpool on hosted for the cutover weekend.
- Data plane: no database schema changes or data migrations involved; routing switch only. Ensure the client library can tolerate 409s/retries for up to 60s.

Validation instrumentation (we'll ship a metrics bundle you can drop into your Prometheus):
- Key metrics: p50/p95/p99 latency by route, error rate by model variant, KV hit rate, GPU utilization, OOMs, and request fanout size.
- Logging: sample-capture for 1% of traffic to verify outputs for quality regression checks.

Cutover cadence (high level):
- T-7d: final smoke tests in staging, runbook dry-run with on-call rotation.
- T-2d: deploy dedicated infra and dry-route a small % (5%) of traffic for 4h.
- T-0d (cutover): 02:00–03:00 PST maintenance window, controlled migration in 4 waves (10% → 30% → 60% → 100%) with 30m observation between waves.
- Rollback: immediate on acceptance criteria breach or sustained error spike; execute hot rollback and call the incident bridge.

I'll drop the draft runbook link: https://drive.google.com/drive/fake-link/novusai-dedicated-runbook (see 'dedicated-cutover-runbook.pdf' attachment).

Karthik
Head of Solutions | Redwood Inference

Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>

Quick executive note: happy to prioritize a warm hosted pool during cutover to meet the <1h rollback SLA. From a commercial perspective, we can offer a one-time migration credit if NovusAI signs the MA by Dec 3. Procurement, please confirm.

Operational ask: please share an internal on-call list for Dec 4–9 (primary + two backups). We'll provide a staged escalation matrix and an on-site engineer (virtual) for the first 8h of each day of the validation window.

I'll be available on the bridge for the cutover wave gating.

Grace Oconnor

- Current hosted baseline: ~3.2M tokens/day, peak sustained qps ~1.8k for our chat endpoint (mostly 1–2k token responses).
- We expect seasonal spikes up to ~3.5k qps for two hour windows (Black Friday-ish).
- Required uptime SLA during business hours (PST): 99.95% to start.

Ask: Can you provide a concrete validation plan (traffic replay / soak), a recommended GPU fleet mix, and a contingency/rollback cadence for the actual cutover? We aim to do a 48–72h validation run in early Dec and cutover the week after if things look green.

I've looped in procurement (procurement@novusai.com) for any contract-side questions.

Thanks,
Nora Patel
Head of ML Platform
NovusAI

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>

Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-30T10:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Sizing ask for hosted→dedicated migration

Nora/Ava —

Based on the hosted export, preliminary sizing suggestions:
- Baseline fleet: 6x A10G (or equivalent T4x2) worker nodes (8 GPUs total via multi-GPU pods) with model shard + KV cache enabled to hit the 1.8k qps baseline with p95 latency ~120–180ms for 2k-token responses.
- Buffer for spikes: design to absorb +2.0x concurrency for short bursts (autoscale up to 12x A10G equivalent).
- Cost/path trade: recommend keeping a quantized 4-bit variant for non-critical routes — saves ~40% GPU cycles during peak.

Validation plan (48h replay):
1) Stage mirror: replay 72h of production traffic at 1.0x velocity for sanity.
2) Soak test: 24h at 1.5x baseline (sustained) to confirm thermal and throughput characteristics.
3) Spike drills: automated bursts to 2.0x for 15–30min to validate autoscaling and fallback to hosted API if node-level issues occur.

Acceptance gates (short list): p95 latency <250ms, error rate <0.1%, KV hit rate >85%, no OOM events for 48h.

Attachments: capacity_matrix_v1.xlsx (I included per-route recommended GPU mapping) and inference-benchmarks.pdf with kernel-level numbers.

Operational notes: make sure your tokenizers are warmed (prefix cache) and prefer batched requests for small messages. We'll script client-side burst-smoothing if you want.

--
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

Date: 2026-12-01T11:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Sizing ask for hosted→dedicated migration

Nora —

Quick answers and details for the runbook:
Rollback timing and impact:
- Hot rollback to hosted: target 20–45 minutes end-to-end for DNS cutover + client-side retry window, assuming the hosted endpoints are warm. We'll keep an active warmpool on hosted for the cutover weekend.
- Data plane: no database schema changes or data migrations involved; routing switch only. Ensure the client library can tolerate 409s/retries for up to 60s.

Validation instrumentation (we'll ship a metrics bundle you can drop into your Prometheus):
- Key metrics: p50/p95/p99 latency by route, error rate by model variant, KV hit rate, GPU utilization, OOMs, and request fanout size.
- Logging: sample-capture for 1% of traffic to verify outputs for quality regression checks.

Cutover cadence (high level):
- T-7d: final smoke tests in staging, runbook dry-run with on-call rotation.
- T-2d: deploy dedicated infra and dry-route a small % (5%) of traffic for 4h.
- T-0d (cutover): 02:00–03:00 PST maintenance window, controlled migration in 4 waves (10% → 30% → 60% → 100%) with 30m observation between waves.
- Rollback: immediate on acceptance criteria breach or sustained error spike; execute hot rollback and call the incident bridge.

I'll drop the draft runbook link: https://drive.google.com/drive/fake-link/novusai-dedicated-runbook (see 'dedicated-cutover-runbook.pdf' attachment).

Karthik
Head of Solutions | Redwood Inference

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the demo earlier — our procurement team asked me to confirm a few constraints before we can move to a PO:

- Our standard net term is net60 for software vendors in EMEA APAC.
- We require a single master PO that references monthly invoices rather than per-invoice POs.
- We need the invoice cadence to be consolidated at month-end; line-item bundling is preferred.
- For discounts: we can only accept slab discounts tied to committed annual spend; prefer % tiers on annualized run-rate.

Can Redwood provide a template PO we can populate and confirm whether you can accept net60, and if there are any holdback or early-pay credits we should be aware of? If not, can you outline alternatives that your finance team will accept?

Attached is our procurement form for vendor fields.

Thanks — we’re trying to line this up with our Q1 vendor onboarding.

Best,
Samantha Price

[Attachment: Novaware_PO_Form.docx]

- Is the net60 a hard requirement for signature, or negotiable if an early-pay credit is applied?
- When you say \"single master PO\" referencing monthly invoices, do you need a PO per legal entity or a single enterprise PO that references invoice months?
- Any mandatory PO fields we must populate (tax IDs, BU codes, project codes)? If you prefer, Vanessa can send our standard PO template back with the fields prefilled.

I’ve looped Vanessa (sales operations) and Karthik (finance) — Vanessa, can you draft a PO template and call out options for monthly consolidation vs itemized invoices? Karthik, please flag the discount and net-term trade-offs: we usually provide deeper up-front discounts for net30 or prepay; net60 requires either reduced discount or short-term surcharge.

Ben

Attached is a tailored PO template and a short summary of options for Novaware (I annotated the fields Novaware might want to keep for ERP matching). Quick summary of our commercial positions relevant to your asks:

Options we can offer (summary):
- Net30 + 7% tiered discount ladder on first-year committed spend (higher discount for larger commit).
- Net60 acceptance with a 2% surcharge OR preserve a reduced discount (3% floor) — whichever you prefer; Net60 removes eligibility for highest slab discounts.
- Monthly consolidated invoicing: we can issue a single month-end invoice that references the master PO and includes bundled SKUs. We will populate an \"Invoice Reference\" field with the PO number to help ERP reconciliation.
- If Novaware can agree to a 60-day payment window but provide a single annual commitment (signed SOW), we can preserve a mid-level discount tier.

Attached: proposed_po_template.pdf (fillable), and a short redlined quote for DEAL-8811.

Suggested next step: if Novaware needs net60 as policy, let us know whether you prefer the 2% surcharge path or the reduced-discount path and we’ll convert the commercial quote into a signature-ready doc. Karthik, please add anything I missed on the finance side.

Thanks,
Vanessa Ortiz
Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: proposed_po_template.pdf]

From: Samantha Price <samantha.price@novaware.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.example>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-12-01T13:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Initial procurement constraints for upcoming Redwood deployment

Thanks Vanessa — the template looks good and the annotations are helpful. A couple of follow-ups from procurement: 

- Net60 is company policy for vendors in our APAC and EMEA entities. It’s negotiable only in exceptional cases; procurement prefers not to move it.
- The 2% surcharge is workable, but our CFO prefers a holdback approach for large SaaS commitments: a 5% holdback released after 90 days of successful usage/uptime SLAs. Would Redwood accept holdback instead of surcharge?
- We also need the invoice to include the following fields to map to our ERP: external BU code (Novaware-BU-12), PO line mapping, and a single consolidated statement at month-end.

If you can put these terms into a revised quote we can present to Legal/Finance next week, that would keep timelines tight. I’ve attached our procurement form again highlighting mandatory fields.

Best,
Samantha
[Attachment: Novaware_PO_Form.docx]

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.example>
To: Samantha Price <samantha.price@novaware.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.example>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-12-02T16:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Procurement details - SKU bundling + net terms and invoice cadence (final)

Samantha — thanks for the clarity and for attaching the required fields. Proposal (final commercial path we can accept for DEAL-8811):

- Net terms: net60 accepted.
- Holdback: 5% holdback retained on each monthly invoice and released after 90 days conditional on uptime & usage thresholds in the SOW (we will include precise SLA metrics in the SOW).
- Discounts: we will offer a reduced annual slab discount structure to keep pricing predictable: 5% at $200k ARR, 7% at $500k ARR, 10% at $1M ARR (annualized). Because of net60 and the holdback, the top slab is limited unless Novaware executes an annual prepay.
- Invoice cadence: monthly consolidated invoice at month-end referencing a single master PO number (Novaware-BU-12 in the invoice header). We will include PO line mappings and a summary CSV with line items for ERP ingestion.
- Surcharge: none. The holdback covers the credit risk; Redwood will not apply the 2% surcharge if the holdback approach is used.

Actions:
1) Vanessa will issue a revised signature-ready quote (Redwood-quote-8811-v3.pdf) that reflects the above terms and includes the PO template fields you requested.
2) Samantha to confirm formally whether Novaware accepts the 5% holdback + net60 path; upon confirmation Redwood will return the signed quote for Legal.
3) Karthik to confirm accounting code mappings if Novaware needs any additional tax fields.

If that works, I’ll have Vanessa send the revised quote and we can aim for countersignature next week.

Thanks — appreciate the collaboration.

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood-quote-8811-v3.pdf]
